findings.description: 10 registered exception handlers covering all error categories: validation (400), authorization (403), not-found (404), concurrency conflict (409), rate limiting (429), backpressure (429), DAPR unavailable (503), and global fallback (500). All return ProblemDetails with Retry-After headers where appropriate. Advisory status writes (non-blocking) prevent cascade failures.

OpenTelemetry SDK fully integrated: 3 custom ActivitySources, 11 span names, OTLP exporter, JSON structured logging with LoggerMessage attributes (10+ EventIds), correlation ID middleware, CloudEvents trace propagation. Health checks: 3 endpoints (/health, /alive, /ready) with 4 DAPR component probes.
recommendations: MINOR: Add dynamic log level toggle (currently requires restart for log level changes)
OPTIONAL: Add custom metrics endpoint (/metrics) for Prometheus scraping

findings.description: Persist-then-publish state machine (ActorStateMachine.cs) with checkpoint stages ensures crash recovery. DAPR resiliency policies: 3 retry policies (constant/exponential), 2 circuit breakers, 3 timeout policies. Dead-letter routing for infrastructure failures. Event drain recovery via IRemindable for unpublished events after pub/sub outage.

Zero data loss architecture (persist-then-publish, deterministic replay, checkpointed recovery) but RTO/RPO targets are UNDEFINED. No backup/restore procedures tested. No failover drills documented. Admin tooling provides backup/restore UI but no automated DR tested.
evidence: PRD NFR22: Zero data loss (architecture supports this via persist-then-publish)
PRD NFR23: Deterministic replay from checkpoint (implemented in ActorStateMachine)
PRD NFR24: Pub/sub recovery via DAPR retry policies (configured in resiliency.yaml)
GAP: No RTO/RPO targets defined in PRD or architecture doc
GAP: No backup restore test evidence
recommendations: HIGH: Define RTO and RPO targets (e.g., RTO <4h, RPO <1h)
MEDIUM: Document and practice failover drills quarterly
MEDIUM: Add chaos engineering tests (state store crash, pub/sub outage) to Tier 3

findings.description: 99.9% availability target (NFR21) defined but unvalidated. Architecture supports HA via DAPR multi-replica + actor placement. Health probes configured for Kubernetes (startup, readiness, liveness). No historical uptime data or incident response procedures documented.
evidence: Kubernetes health probes defined (startup 60s, readiness /ready, liveness /alive)
DAPR placement service for actor distribution
NO uptime monitoring or SLA tracking in production
recommendations: MEDIUM: Set up uptime monitoring (e.g., Grafana SLO dashboards)

priority_actions: HIGH: Define RTO/RPO targets and test backup/restore procedure
HIGH: Validate 99.9% availability architecture under failure scenarios
MEDIUM: Add chaos engineering tests to Tier 3 suite
MINOR: Add dynamic log level toggle and /metrics endpoint
summary: Reliability posture is LOW risk for core operations — excellent error handling (10 handlers), full observability (OTel + structured logs + health checks), robust fault tolerance (persist-then-publish + DAPR resiliency). CONCERNS around disaster recovery (undefined RTO/RPO, untested backup/restore) and availability validation (99.9% target untested).
